qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page provides details of completed events that are part of the infrastructure renewal activities.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system is being introduced as part of the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: The new system being introduced is Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Niagara cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The Niagara cluster was fully decommissioned on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Mist cluster decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The Mist cluster was decommissioned on September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What initial capacity reductions did Niagara and Mist experience starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara operated at approximately 50% capacity, and Mist at approximately 35% capacity to support system improvements and transition to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: When was Trillium Open OnDemand launched?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium Open OnDemand was launched on September 11, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham compute cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham compute cluster was fully decommissioned on September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: How did Graham operate during its reduction period starting February 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham operated at approximately 25% capacity under a simplified scheduling configuration with limited runtimes and support for CPU and GPU jobs.

qa_pairs.question: Was Graham Cloud affected during the Graham compute cluster reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Graham Cloud remained fully operational throughout the reduction period.

qa_pairs.question: What services were unavailable during the tape storage system migration from July 15 to August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: File backup and restore services, along with the TSM service itself, were unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the status of data created or modified between July 15 and August 11, 2025, during the tape storage outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Data created or modified during this specific period could not be recovered.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Fir cluster capacity reduced from August 25 to September 5, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The reduction supported the commissioning of new cooling equipment.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Rorqual compute cluster fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: The Rorqual cluster was fully unavailable on September 3, 2025, from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. EDT.

qa_pairs.question: What was Cedar's operating capacity starting January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster operated at approximately 70% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the extended Cedar outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was for critical data-centre power and cooling upgrades for new system installation, extended due to an unexpected cooling tower failure.

qa_pairs.question: How was file system access handled during Cedar's extended outage in June 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: File systems were accessible read-only via Globus, with access limited to the old project file system during the week of June 23.

qa_pairs.question: What systems were affected by scheduled electrical maintenance outages on April 30-May 1, June 6-10, and June 13-16, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga and Narval compute nodes, and Juno Cloud instances in the non-High Availability zone, were affected.

qa_pairs.question: During the electrical maintenance outages for Béluga and Narval, was their storage accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible via Globus and login nodes, though with intermittent disruptions during the June 6-10 outage.

qa_pairs.question: Did Cedar Cloud remain operational during the March 31 – April 2, 2025, outage for power modifications?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar Cloud remained operational during this period.

qa_pairs.question: What was the initial and extended duration of the complete Graham data centre shutdown?
qa_pairs.answer: It was originally scheduled from December 7, 2024, to January 3, 2025, and then extended to February 24, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What Graham services were unavailable during the complete data centre shutdown from December 2024 to February 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: All Graham cluster services, storage, and cloud services were entirely unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the temporary reduction of Béluga and Narval compute nodes in January 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Performance and stability tests on Rorqual required the shutdown of all Béluga compute nodes and about half of Narval compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What specific file system migration occurred during the Cedar compute cluster shutdown from January 13-21, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar /scratch filesystem was migrated to new storage.

qa_pairs.question: Why did Niagara experience a full power shutdown on November 25-26, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: It was for main panel upgrades ahead of Trillium cluster setup.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems at the SciNet Datacenter were unavailable on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara, Mist, HPSS, Rouge, Teach, JupyterHub, and Balam were all unavailable.
